hero.eyebrow: Sustainable tourism for Ham Ninh fishing village
hero.title: Ham Ninh AI Guide — Preserve Heritage. Support Tourism. Fairness for Fishermen.
hero.description: A multi-agent AI concept combining cultural-preservation RAG and ensemble re-ranking to support local livelihoods in Ham Ninh fishing village, Phu Quoc.

trustBadges.label: Fair re-ranking
trustBadges.description: Adds local business signals to recommendation order.

trustBadges.label: Bilingual content
trustBadges.description: Vietnamese and English content share the same structure.

problem.heading: Tourism can grow quickly while local value is left behind
problem.body: When large platforms optimize for popularity, Ham Ninh’s cultural stories and small local businesses need a fairer introduction layer.

cards.title: Over-tourism & cultural erosion
cards.description: Fragmented information can reduce the village to photo spots without explaining its fishing heritage.

cards.title: Platform economic bias
cards.description: Businesses with more reviews or marketing budgets often outrank local family-run providers.

cards.title: Missing accurate local sources
cards.description: Cultural answers should come from retrieved documents and citations, not model guesswork.

solution.heading: A multi-agent system connecting culture, maps, and algorithmic fairness
solution.body: The landing page introduces the target architecture: RAG for local knowledge, a Maps Agent for place data, and an Ensemble Re-ranker for responsible ranking.

pillars.title: RAG Agent — Local Guide
pillars.description: Answers culture, history, and food questions using retrieved sources.

pillars.title: Maps & Service Agent — Concierge
pillars.description: Planned to connect with Places API for places, routes, and accessibility context.

pillars.title: Ensemble Re-ranker — Fairness Engine
pillars.description: Combines 3 pre-defined rule-based decision trees via bagging, plus 2 correction stumps, to balance quality, distance, and local_factor — no training required.

responsibleAI.heading: Five control axes from data to user experience
responsibleAI.body: Each axis maps to a metric or verification mechanism that reduces risk as chat and map functionality arrive in later slices.

axes.title: Reliability
axes.description: Cultural answers should stay grounded in retrieved sources and cite them.

axes.title: Bias & fairness
axes.description: Place ranking considers local_factor to support local businesses.

axes.title: Robustness
axes.description: Guardrails, topic filters, and fallbacks reduce dependence on a single failure point.

axes.title: Social impact
axes.description: Prioritizes small vendors, elders, and accessibility needs.

axes.title: Explainability
axes.description: Reasoning logs, citations, and score breakdowns help users understand recommendations.

algorithmShowcase.heading: Ensemble re-ranking turns place signals into a fairer order
algorithmShowcase.body: Shows how 3 pre-defined rule-based decision trees are combined via bagging and correction stumps into a final explainable score — no training required.

chart.title: Illustrative contribution to Final Score
chart.ariaLabel: Bar chart showing example contributions of local factor, rating, and distance to the final score.
chart.description: The values below are interface explanation data, not live Places API results.

bars.label: Tree 1: Locality-first
bars.description: First decision tree scores based on how strongly a place supports local community and livelihoods.

bars.label: Tree 2: Proximity-first
bars.description: Second decision tree prioritizes places near the user's location or planned route.

bars.label: Tree 3: Quality-first
bars.description: Third decision tree evaluates quality from ratings and visitor feedback.

bars.label: Bagging: Average of 3 trees
bars.description: Combines all 3 trees via bagging to reduce variance and increase stability.

bars.label: Fairness adjustment (penalty for chains)
bars.description: Penalty applied to chain businesses (η × -0.15) to reduce their ranking advantage and support small local vendors.

bars.label: Accessibility adjustment (+3%)
bars.description: Bonus for places with high accessibility, supporting visitors with disabilities.

bars.label: Final score
bars.description: The final ensemble result — combining bagged tree scores with fairness and accessibility adjustments.

steps.title: Decision Tree
steps.description: Pre-defined thresholds over rating, distance, and local_factor — each tree applies a simple priority rule.

steps.title: Bagging: Average of 3 trees
steps.description: Averages all 3 hand-written trees via simple mean to stabilize scores and reduce single-tree variance.

steps.title: Correction stumps: fairness + accessibility
steps.description: Applies 2 expert-defined corrections: fairness penalty for chain businesses, bonus for accessibility features.

steps.title: Final score: clip and rank
steps.description: Clips the result to [0,1] and assigns a 1-based rank — fully explainable, no training needed.

techStack.heading: Academic stack, designed for real operation
techStack.body: The components support i18n, knowledge retrieval, agent orchestration, RAG evaluation, and system observability.

items.name: Next.js 16
items.description: Frontend App Router, locale routes, and responsive landing UI.

items.name: FastAPI
items.description: API gateway for chat, SSE, and health checks.

items.name: LangGraph
items.description: Supervisor and worker agent orchestration with checkpoints.

items.name: Qdrant
items.description: Vector database for Ham Ninh cultural RAG.

items.name: Goong Maps Platform
items.description: Place and routing data source in later slices.

items.name: Pre-defined Ensemble
items.description: Hand-written decision trees with bagging and correction stumps for explainable re-ranking — no ML training.

items.name: RAGAS
items.description: Evaluates faithfulness, relevance, and context recall.

items.name: Langfuse
items.description: Traces, latency, and cost for agent workflows.

demo.heading: The experience shell opens progressively by slice
demo.body: In this slice, the landing page describes target capabilities. Real chat, map, and architecture functionality will be connected in later slices.
demo.note: CTAs may lead to scaffold or in-progress pages; they do not imply fully working chat/map behavior before S03.

items.title: Citation-backed chat
items.description: Planned to show RAG answers with sources and a reasoning log.

items.title: Re-ranked map
items.description: Planned to show Places API results after fairness scoring.

items.title: Transparent architecture
items.description: Planned to explain the supervisor, agents, guardrails, and observability.

Chat.title: Ham Ninh Assistant
Chat.placeholder: Ask naturally: say hi, find food, get directions, explore culture...
Chat.error: Unable to reach the assistant. Please try again.
Chat.noEvidence: I couldn't find information about that. Try asking differently.
Chat.welcomeGreeting: Plan Ham Ninh with an assistant that chats like a real guide
Chat.welcomeSubtitle: Start with small talk or ask directly. The assistant answers naturally and only reaches for sources when your question needs them.
prompts: Give me a short village history
How do I get there from Duong Dong?

Map.title: Ham Ninh Place Proof Map
Map.intro: Inspect live place recommendations returned by the backend agent on a real Goong map: result cards, selected details, synchronized pins, and neutral source links.
Map.defaultQuery: Recommend local seafood restaurants and family-run places near Ham Ninh fishing village with accessibility details.
Map.searchPlaceholder: Ask for places or services in Ham Ninh...
Map.unavailable: The recommendation service is unavailable right now.
Map.fallback: The assistant used a fallback response:
Map.pinUnavailable: Pin unavailable: coordinates were not returned for this place.
Map.missingMapToken: Goong map tiles are not configured. Results remain available as cards until NEXT_PUBLIC_GOONG_MAPTILES_KEY is set.
Map.mapUnavailable: The Goong map could not initialize. Result cards and place details are still available.
Map.noPins: No map pins can be drawn because these results do not include usable coordinates.

overview.heading: Multi-agent AI assistant for Ham Ninh fishing village
overview.body: The architecture combines Retrieval-Augmented Generation for cultural knowledge, Google Places API for location intelligence, ensemble re-ranking for fairness, and LangGraph-based agent orchestration — all served through a bilingual Next.js frontend with full observability.

cards.title: Data Flow
cards.description: User → Frontend → Agent Orchestrator → Workers (RAG, Maps) → SSE Streaming → Response

ragPipeline.heading: RAG Pipeline — Citation-backed cultural Q&A
ragPipeline.body: Answers about Ham Ninh's culture, history, and cuisine are grounded in retrieved documents — not model guesswork. Every answer includes citations and is evaluated by RAGAS for faithfulness and relevance.

cards.title: Document Corpus
cards.description: Cultural documents are normalized, chunked, and embedded into a Qdrant vector store for fast similarity search.

cards.title: Vector Search
cards.description: User queries are embedded and matched against the corpus using top-k similarity with configurable thresholds.

cards.title: Grounded Answers
cards.description: The LLM generates answers anchored in retrieved context, with inline citations back to source documents.

mapsApi.heading: Maps & Places API — Real-world place data
mapsApi.body: Google Places API (New) provides nearby search, text search, place details, and autocomplete. Routes API powers computeRouteMatrix for distance-aware recommendations. A circuit breaker ensures graceful fallback when the API is unavailable.

cards.title: Place Discovery
cards.description: Nearby search and text search find restaurants, services, and cultural sites near Ham Ninh fishing village.

cards.title: Route Planning
cards.description: computeRouteMatrix calculates distances between user location and candidate places for proximity-aware ranking.

cards.title: Fallback Handling
cards.description: Circuit breaker detects API failures and returns curated fallback results instead of error pages.

ensembleReranker.heading: Ensemble Re-ranking — Fair and explainable scoring
ensembleReranker.body: Three pre-defined rule-based decision trees (Locality-first, Proximity-first, Quality-first) are combined via bagging. Two correction stumps apply fairness penalties for chains and accessibility bonuses. The local_factor ensures ≥40% local businesses appear in the top-5 results.

cards.title: Decision Trees
cards.description: Locality-first, Proximity-first, and Quality-first trees score places using hand-written rules — no training required.

cards.title: Correction Stumps
cards.description: Fairness penalty reduces chain business rankings; accessibility bonus rewards places with disability-friendly features.

cards.title: Fairness Constraint
cards.description: The local_factor boosts local businesses so at least 40% of top-5 results are community-run establishments.

orchestration.heading: Agent Orchestration — LangGraph supervisor pattern
orchestration.body: A LangGraph supervisor coordinates specialized worker agents: the RAG Agent for cultural answers and the Maps & Service Agent for place data. Guardrails filter unsafe inputs, and SSE streaming delivers real-time chat responses with session durability.

cards.title: Supervisor Pattern
cards.description: The supervisor routes queries to the right worker agent based on intent — RAG for culture, Maps for places.

cards.title: Streaming Responses
cards.description: Server-Sent Events (SSE) deliver token-level streaming for a responsive chat experience.

cards.title: Session State
cards.description: LangGraph checkpoints preserve conversation history and agent state across session boundaries.

frontendShell.heading: Frontend Shell & Observability
frontendShell.body: Next.js 16 App Router powers the frontend with next-intl for bilingual support (English/Vietnamese). Langfuse traces agent workflows, health endpoints monitor system status, and responsive UI ensures accessibility across devices.

cards.title: App Shell
cards.description: Next.js 16 with App Router, responsive layout, and progressive enhancement from landing page to full chat/map.

cards.title: i18n Support
cards.description: next-intl provides locale routing, message files, and server-side translation for all UI text in English and Vietnamese.

cards.title: System Observability
cards.description: Langfuse traces, health endpoints, and structured logging provide visibility into agent performance and system health.
